subject: Human Resources
title: Structured vs. Unstructured Interviews
description: A fast breakdown of Structured vs. Unstructured Interviews for builders.

hook.text: Two interviewers assess the same candidate. One loves them. The other rejects them. Neither asked the same questions.

buildup.text: Unstructured interviews—casual conversations—are the most common format but among the worst predictors of success.

discovery.text: Structured interviews ask every candidate the same questions, scored against consistent criteria.

twist.text: Research shows structured interviews are twice as predictive of job performance as unstructured ones.

climax.text: Structure doesn't mean robotic—it means fair, consistent, and evidence-based while still feeling human.

punchline.text: Same questions, same criteria. Fairness requires structure.

quiz.question: Why are structured interviews superior?
options: They're twice as predictive of job performance
